content.problemStatement:
It's the soccer match finals in Chefland and as always it has reached the penalty shootouts.
Each team is given 555 shots to make and the team scoring a goal on the maximum number of shots wins the game. If both the teams' scores are equal, then the game is considered a draw and we would have 222 champions.

Given ten integers A1,A2,…,A10A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{10}A1​,A2​,…,A10​, where the odd indexed integers(A1,A3,A_1, A_3,A1​,A3​, A5,A_5,A5​, A7,A9A_7, A_9A7​,A9​) represent the outcome of the shots made by team 111 and even indexed integers(A2,A4,A6,A8,A10A_2, A_4, A_6, A_8, A_{10}A2​,A4​,A6​,A8​,A10​) represent the outcome of the shots made by team 222 (here Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 indicates that it's a goal and Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 indicates a miss), determine the winner or find if the game ends in a draw.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains ten space-separated integers A1,A2,…,A10A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{10}A1​,A2​,…,A10​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - 000 if the game ends in a draw or 111 if the first team wins or 222 if the second team wins.
constraints: Exactly ten integers are given per test case
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains exactly ten space‑separated integers A1 … A10, where Ai is either 0 or 1. Odd‑indexed values (A1,A3,A5,A7,A9) are goals of team 1, even‑indexed values (A2,A4,A6,A8,A10) are goals of team 2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: 0 if both teams score the same number of goals, 1 if team 1 scores more, or 2 if team 2 scores more.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: both teams score 0 → draw (0).
Test case 2: only team 2 scores in the last shot → team 2 wins (2).
Test case 3: team 1 scores twice, team 2 scores none → team 1 wins (1).
Test case 4: team 1 scores 5, team 2 scores 4 → team 1 wins (1).

solution.approach: For each test case read the ten values. Accumulate the sum of the five odd‑positioned numbers (team 1) and the sum of the five even‑positioned numbers (team 2). Compare the two sums and output 0, 1 or 2 accordingly. The algorithm runs in O(10) per test case, i.e., O(T) overall, and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – ten constant‑time operations per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
